sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: Let${ A}$and${ B}$be subvarieties of a variety of groups${ M}$; then$({ A}{ B}) { M}$is called the${ M$-pro\-duct of${ A}$by${ B}$\/}, where${ A}{ B}$is the usual product. Does there exist a non-abelian variety${ M}$with an infinite lattice of subvarieties and commutative${ M}$-multi\-pli\-ca\-tion? { A.\,I.\,Mal'cev} Yes, there does; for example,${ M}={ A}_p{ A}_p$, where${ A}_p$is the variety of...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
